mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which compresses near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and payroll employment indicate a broad-based contraction in economic activity, reflecting weakening aggregate demand that suppresses corporate revenue growth and equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between joint contraction and expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and expanding residential credit availability signal a stabilization in the housing sector, which historically leads positive equity returns as consumer wealth effects and construction activity boost aggregate demand.
failure_condition: Housing starts lead equity returns by less than 5 trading days, or the correlation between housing permits and SPX forward returns drops below 0.1 over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate acts as a fundamental earnings driver, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer investment horizon as corporate profitability improves.
failure_condition: GDP growth surprises fail to explain more than 5% of the variance in 60-day forward returns, or the signal generates negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month inflation readings trigger market fears of premature monetary tightening, causing short-term equity drawdowns as investors reprice discount rates and risk premiums upward.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally on inflation surprises exceeding the threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for Federal Reserve communication events.

mechanism_narrative: High baseline unemployment combined with negative payroll revisions confirms labor market fragility, amplifying downside equity risk as wage growth stagnates and consumer spending power erodes over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high-unemployment/negative-payroll and high-unemployment/positive-payroll regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rebounding industrial production reflects pent-up demand fulfillment and supply chain normalization, driving short-term equity rallies as corporate capacity utilization improves margins.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds fail to translate into positive equity returns within 5 trading days, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
